opportunities.title: AI-Powered Integrated Operations Center (IOC) Automation
opportunities.planesense_pain: 24/7 IOC is entirely human-staffed — crew scheduling, reservations, weather rerouting, catering, client preferences, slot management. Rapid program expansion (Jetfly, CobaltPass, Sourcing Solution) is compounding coordination load.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Calfus AI agents to automate IOC workflows: intelligent crew-aircraft-trip matching, weather-triggered rerouting suggestions, preference-based auto-configuration, partner flight sourcing (Jetfly/CaptainJet) orchestration. Parallels Calfus AssistIQ for ticket triage.
opportunities.business_case: 60+ aircraft, 240+ pilots, 40+ bases, 37,300+ missions/year — even 10% automation of IOC decisions saves significant manual hours and improves response quality at scale.
opportunities.entry_point: Start with a scoped AI proof-of-concept on crew scheduling or weather-triggered rerouting — measurable, bounded, fast ROI.
opportunities.buyer: Kevin Gordon (VP Flight Operations) + Mandar Pendse (CIO)

opportunities.title: Oracle Cloud ERP — Multi-Entity Financial Management
opportunities.calfus_capability: ERP Agent Foundry / Oracle Cloud ERP
opportunities.planesense_pain: PlaneSense is a 30-year-old company with 600 employees, 60+ aircraft, two legal entities (PlaneSense + Atlas Aircraft Center), and a rapidly growing web of international financial relationships (Jetfly hour reconciliation, CaptainJet charter billing, Sourcing Solution external operators). Zero ERP platform mention in all scraped content.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Oracle Cloud ERP implementation covering: fractional share asset management, owner billing (management fees + hourly), intercompany reconciliation (PlaneSense ↔ Atlas), partner billing (Jetfly, CaptainJet), multi-currency international transactions, maintenance cost allocation, fleet depreciation tracking.
opportunities.business_case: Three new product lines in 2025 + two new international partnerships + West Coast expansion = finance team complexity growing exponentially. Inability to reconcile cross-entity and cross-border financials cleanly is a real risk at this scale.
opportunities.entry_point: Lead with financial complexity of Jetfly partnership — 'How are you currently reconciling cross-border fractional hours and billing between your owners and Jetfly owners?' This surfaces the gap naturally.
opportunities.buyer: Jim Citro (CFO) + George Antoniadis (CEO for final approval)

opportunities.title: Predictive Maintenance & Fleet Intelligence Platform
opportunities.planesense_pain: 60+ aircraft fleet, 194,000+ parts inventory, two maintenance facilities, documented parts supply chain vulnerabilities ('shipping delays impacting return-to-service'). Fleet-wide upgrade programs simultaneously underway. FAA Diamond Award compliance requires zero-defect documentation.
opportunities.proposed_solution: AI-driven predictive maintenance layer for Atlas Aircraft Center: component lifecycle forecasting, parts demand prediction, supplier lead time optimization, technician scheduling, maintenance window sequencing to minimize aircraft downtime, compliance documentation automation.
opportunities.business_case: A single aircraft out of service for an unplanned maintenance event disrupts owner scheduling guarantees. With 60+ aircraft and a 91% retention rate as the brand promise, unplanned downtime is a direct revenue and reputation risk. Predictive maintenance directly protects that promise.
opportunities.entry_point: Atlas Aircraft Center is a separate legal entity — this can be a standalone engagement independent of the main PlaneSense conversation.
opportunities.buyer: Todd Smith (Director of Airworthiness) + Atlas leadership

opportunities.title: Owner Digital Experience Platform
opportunities.planesense_pain: Owner mobile app confirmed (basic scheduling only). No owner portal described — no self-service for trip history, invoices, preference management, or digital onboarding. West Coast expansion and Jetfly partnership are bringing in new owner profiles who expect modern digital experiences.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Full-stack owner experience platform: enhanced mobile app (real-time flight tracking, intelligent scheduling with conflict detection, preference management), web portal (digital invoicing, flight history, share management, document access), onboarding flow for new owners, integrated with HubSpot (existing CRM).
opportunities.business_case: 91% retention is driven by service quality — but modern clients increasingly benchmark their experience against consumer apps (Uber, Delta app, etc.). Keeping pace digitally is a retention and acquisition competitive advantage, especially for the younger-generation owners being recruited.
opportunities.entry_point: 'What does your owner app currently support, and what are your plans to expand it?' — lets them identify the gap themselves.
opportunities.buyer: Mandar Pendse (CIO) + Danielle McPheters (Director, Account Services)

opportunities.title: Data Intelligence & Operational Analytics
opportunities.planesense_pain: Massive operational data generated (37K+ missions/year, 1,343 airports, fleet telemetry, maintenance records, pilot performance, weather events, client preferences, pricing data) with zero visible analytics layer. CobaltPass sold out — suggests demand forecasting gap. No mention of BI tools, dashboards, or data-driven decision making in any content.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Unified data intelligence platform: operational dashboards (fleet utilization, mission completion rates, IOC performance), revenue analytics (owner segment profitability, program mix, pricing optimization), demand forecasting (CobaltPass inventory, seasonal peaks), pilot performance analytics, maintenance cost intelligence.
opportunities.business_case: CobaltPass selling out within months of launch signals unmet demand they cannot currently quantify or plan for. A data layer would directly improve capacity planning, pricing decisions, and product development cadence.
opportunities.entry_point: 'How do you currently forecast demand for CobaltPass seats — and how did you arrive at the initial inventory size?' This surfaces the forecasting gap directly.
opportunities.buyer: Mandar Pendse (CIO) + Jim Citro (CFO for revenue analytics)

opportunities.title: AssistIQ for Pilot & Operations Support Triage
opportunities.planesense_pain: IOC supports pilots from takeoff to landing, 24/7. Owner inquiries, maintenance tickets, flight changes, and weather events all generate support volume simultaneously. No visible ticketing or triage system mentioned.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Deploy Calfus AssistIQ to: triage IOC support requests by urgency/type, automate common owner inquiry responses (flight status, billing questions, program details), route complex issues to right specialist, continuously learn from resolutions to improve efficiency.
opportunities.business_case: As PlaneSense adds CobaltPass, Sourcing Solution, and Jetfly coordination, IOC support volume will grow non-linearly. AssistIQ contains that cost growth while improving response time — a direct retention-quality play.
opportunities.entry_point: Reference AssistIQ by name with the Oracle connection — 'We have a product called AssistIQ originally built for Oracle support operations that has direct parallels to aviation IOC operations.'
opportunities.buyer: Kevin Gordon (VP Flight Operations) + Mandar Pendse (CIO)

opportunities.title: Jetfly / Partner Integration Layer
opportunities.planesense_pain: Jetfly partnership (April 2025) requires seamless scheduling, flight hour reconciliation, and client experience continuity between two independent organizations operating in different regulatory jurisdictions. CaptainJet adds a third integration point. Currently described with no technology infrastructure detail — likely being managed manually.
opportunities.proposed_solution: API integration layer connecting PlaneSense IOC and owner systems with Jetfly scheduling and Calfus/CaptainJet sourcing workflows. Automated cross-border flight hour tracking, reconciliation, and billing. Unified client experience regardless of which partner operates the flight.
opportunities.business_case: The Jetfly partnership was launched in April 2025 — it is less than a year old and being managed at scale manually. As usage grows, manual reconciliation will break down. Building the integration now is far cheaper than fixing a crisis later.
opportunities.entry_point: 'Who is your current technology contact at Jetfly, and how are you tracking cross-fleet flight hours today?' — immediately surfaces whether there is a system or not.
opportunities.buyer: Mike Baur (VP Business Development & Strategy) + Mandar Pendse (CIO)

priority_ranking: OPP-01: IOC AI Automation — immediate pain, visible complexity, daily operational impact
OPP-02: Oracle Cloud ERP — strategic, CFO-level, Jetfly is the perfect entry wedge
OPP-03: Predictive Maintenance (Atlas) — standalone Atlas engagement, separate buyer
OPP-04: Owner Digital Platform — software engineering, retention play, CIO-driven
OPP-05: Data Intelligence — analytics layer, underpins all other opportunities
OPP-06: AssistIQ IOC Triage — product demo opportunity, fast to show value
OPP-07: Jetfly Integration — immediate and growing, but depends on Jetfly cooperation
recommended_meeting_focus: Lead with OPP-01 (IOC automation) and OPP-03 (Atlas maintenance) to show operational understanding
Pivot to OPP-02 (ERP / Jetfly billing) to surface financial complexity
Close with OPP-06 (AssistIQ demo offer) as a fast, concrete next step
